article_title: IMO's Shipping Net-Zero Framework Heads to Crucial April Session After US-Led Blockade Delayed Adoption by a Year

content_preview.title: IMO's Shipping Net-Zero Framework Heads to Crucial April Session After US-Led Blockade Delayed Adoption by a Year
content_preview.summary: MEPC 84 convenes April 27 in London for the first regular session since a US-Saudi coalition blocked adoption of the first global carbon pricing system for shipping in October 2025.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The International Maritime Organization's Marine Environment Protection Committee will convene its 84th session (MEPC 84) in London from April 27 to May 1, marking the first regular meeting since an extraordinary session in October 2025 ended in a bitter adjournment over the proposed Net-Zero Framework. The framework, which would establish the world's first mandatory carbon pricing and fuel-intensity reduction system for an entire industry, was approved in principle at MEPC 83 in Ap...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive climate/shipping policy article with excellent use of 5 sources including UN, Euronews, Carbon Brief, and DOE.
editor_notes.source_verification: Euronews confirms 57-49 vote, US-led blockade October 2025, Trump quote, postponement. Carbon Brief confirms $380/unit fee, $11-12B annual revenue, emission targets, two-tiered penalty structure. Both UN News sources and DOE joint statement are authoritative primary sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified. Vote count (57-49-21 in article vs 57-49 in Euronews — article includes abstentions), fee levels, revenue projections, 87 maritime firms, MEPC 84 April 27 all confirmed or consistent.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced between the opposing positions. No editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior IMO shipping emissions coverage in the archive. Distinct from existing IMO autonomous ship code article.
